title: Zygophyllum bruguieri
taxonomy: Zygophyllum bruguieri is classified in the family Zygophyllaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the jujube or creosote bush family. This family is part of the order Zygophyllales, a small order of flowering plants. The order Zygophyllales is further classified under the clade angiosperms, which includes the majority of flowering plant species. The clade angiosperms is a subset of the larger clade of flowering plants called the embryophytes. Embryophytes are a group of plants that have roots and leaves and reproduce using seeds.

intro: Zygophyllum bruguieri, also known as Desert Thorn or Al-Abah, is a succulent plant species native to the Middle Eastern and North African regions. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it beneficial in treating various health conditions such as wounds, fever, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Zygophyllum bruguieri include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties.
